title: Fundamental Question 1
conciseAnswer: Focus on the 5 main categories: market, credit, operational, liquidity, and strategic risk. Mention how they differ and provide examples for each.

title: Fundamental Question 2
conciseAnswer: Key points: 1) Definition - risk of loss from borrower default 2) Measurement - PD, LGD, EAD 3) Mitigation - collateral, covenants, diversification.

title: Fundamental Question 3
conciseAnswer: Remember VaR basics: 1) What it measures (potential loss at confidence level) 2) Key parameters (confidence, time horizon) 3) Limitations (tail risk, assumptions).

title: Fundamental Question 4
conciseAnswer: Stress testing involves: 1) Designing extreme scenarios 2) Assessing impact on portfolio 3) Informing risk appetite. Mention regulatory requirements and practical applications.

title: Fundamental Question 5
conciseAnswer: Basel framework pillars: 1) Minimum capital requirements 2) Supervisory review 3) Market discipline. Know the progression from Basel I to III/IV.

title: Fundamental Question 6
conciseAnswer: Operational risk categories: people, processes, systems, external events. Measurement approaches: basic indicator, standardized, advanced measurement.

title: Fundamental Question 7
conciseAnswer: Counterparty risk management: exposure measurement, credit limits, collateralization, netting agreements, credit valuation adjustment (CVA).

title: Fundamental Question 8
conciseAnswer: Key distinction: Systematic (market-wide, non-diversifiable) vs. unsystematic (firm-specific, diversifiable). CAPM framework: beta measures systematic risk exposure.

title: Fundamental Question 9
conciseAnswer: Risk appetite framework: 1) Define tolerance levels 2) Align with strategy 3) Communicate across organization 4) Monitor compliance.

title: Fundamental Question 10
conciseAnswer: Risk management supports: 1) Strategic decision-making 2) Capital allocation 3) Regulatory compliance 4) Stakeholder confidence 5) Sustainable growth.

title: Fundamental Question 11
conciseAnswer: Focus on the 5 main categories: market, credit, operational, liquidity, and strategic risk. Mention how they differ and provide examples for each.

conciseAnswer: Key points: 1) Definition - risk of loss from borrower default 2) Measurement - PD, LGD, EAD 3) Mitigation - collateral, covenants, diversification.

title: Fundamental Question 17
conciseAnswer: Counterparty risk management: exposure measurement, credit limits, collateralization, netting agreements, credit valuation adjustment (CVA).

conciseAnswer: Key distinction: Systematic (market-wide, non-diversifiable) vs. unsystematic (firm-specific, diversifiable). CAPM framework: beta measures systematic risk exposure.

title: Fundamental Question 19
conciseAnswer: Risk appetite framework: 1) Define tolerance levels 2) Align with strategy 3) Communicate across organization 4) Monitor compliance.

title: Fundamental Question 20
conciseAnswer: Risk management supports: 1) Strategic decision-making 2) Capital allocation 3) Regulatory compliance 4) Stakeholder confidence 5) Sustainable growth.

title: Model Risk Management - Technical Question
conciseAnswer: Model risk management challenges: model validation, documentation, governance, ongoing monitoring. Address through robust frameworks and independent review.

title: Operational Risk Framework - Behavioral Question
conciseAnswer: Share specific example: process improvement that reduced errors, system implementation that enhanced controls, or incident response that minimized losses.

title: Basel Regulations & Capital Requirements - Case Question
conciseAnswer: Basel principles: risk-based capital, leverage ratio, liquidity requirements. Know the three pillars and their implementation challenges.

title: Liquidity Risk Measurement - Technical Question
conciseAnswer: Liquidity metrics: LCR, NSFR, liquidity gaps, funding concentration. Stress testing for liquidity scenarios is crucial.

title: Enterprise Risk Management - Behavioral Question
conciseAnswer: ERM integrates risk across organization: strategic, operational, financial, compliance risks. Focus on value protection and creation.

title: Risk Appetite Statement - Case Question
conciseAnswer: Risk appetite statement should be: 1) Quantitative and qualitative 2) Board-approved 3) Cascaded to business units 4) Regularly reviewed.

title: Climate Risk Assessment - Technical Question
conciseAnswer: Climate risk approaches: physical (acute/chronic) vs. transition risks. TCFD framework: governance, strategy, risk management, metrics/targets.

title: Value at Risk (VaR) Calculation & Limitations - Behavioral Question
conciseAnswer: Structure with STAR: Situation, Task, Action, Result. Be specific about your role, challenges faced, and impact created.

title: Stress Testing Scenarios - Case Question
conciseAnswer: Scenario design: historical, hypothetical, reverse stress tests. Include market shocks, economic downturns, operational failures.

title: Counterparty Credit Risk - Technical Question
conciseAnswer: Counterparty risk measures: exposure at default, potential future exposure, credit valuation adjustment. Mitigation via collateral, netting, clearing.

title: Model Risk Management - Behavioral Question
conciseAnswer: Explain model risk simply: 'garbage in, garbage out' concept. Emphasize importance of understanding model limitations and assumptions.

title: Operational Risk Framework - Case Question
conciseAnswer: Framework: 1) Analyze the problem 2) Identify key issues 3) Propose solutions 4) Evaluate trade-offs 5) Recommend implementation plan.
